title: Quick Wins
subtitle: Maximum performance impact with minimal effort — can be implemented immediately without release risk.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Data Storage & API Usage reveals which objects hold the most records and where unselective queries are running.

recommendations.title: Custom Indexes on High-Volume Objects
recommendations.body: For objects with >100k records: identify the most frequently used filter fields in SOQL queries and reports. File a case with Salesforce Support to request Custom Indexes on these fields. This eliminates full table scans and dramatically reduces query execution time.

recommendations.title: Consolidate Sharing Rules
recommendations.body: Audit existing Sharing Rules for redundant or overlapping entries. Consolidate where possible and use Sharing Groups instead of individual rules. This drastically reduces share row recalculation time and improves DML performance on shared objects.

recommendations.title: Validation Rules with Early Return
recommendations.body: Review Validation Rules that perform expensive SOQL-based cross-object checks. Place inexpensive field-level checks at the beginning of the rule using AND logic, so costly evaluations only run when necessary. This avoids unnecessary Governor Limit consumption.

title: Prioritize
subtitle: High performance impact with moderate effort — schedule for the next sprint or release cycle.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Apex Executions & Concurrent Requests show how close you are to Apex Governor Limits and where concurrency bottlenecks exist.

recommendations.title: Apex CPU Time Optimization
recommendations.body: Identify the top 5 Apex transactions by CPU time using Debug Logs. Common causes: nested loops, unselective SOQL in Triggers, unnecessary String concatenation. Use Limits.getCpuTime() for in-transaction profiling to isolate expensive code paths.

recommendations.title: Bulkify All Triggers
recommendations.body: Audit all Apex Triggers for proper bulkification. SOQL queries and DML statements must never appear inside loops. Adopt the Trigger Handler pattern using Maps for bulk operations to prevent hitting Governor Limits during data loads and batch operations.

recommendations.title: Row Lock Analysis & Mitigation
recommendations.body: Row Locks occur when parallel DML operations target the same records. Use Scale Center to identify affected objects. Mitigation strategies: offload processing to Queueable Apex, enforce record ordering in batch jobs, and avoid parent record lock escalation through careful DML sequencing.

title: Strategic
subtitle: High performance impact but significant effort required — plan as a strategic initiative with a clear business case.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → cross-metric trends (login growth, storage growth, API usage trajectory) indicate whether a strategic architectural change is needed.

recommendations.title: Adopt Event-Driven Architecture
recommendations.body: For orgs with high inter-system coupling: introduce Platform Events or Change Data Capture as a decoupling layer. This reduces synchronous callouts, improves scalability, and makes the architecture more resilient to downstream system failures.

recommendations.title: Data Architecture Review
recommendations.body: For objects exceeding 10M records: evaluate archival strategies (Big Objects, external storage), data partitioning approaches, and whether a Data Model refactoring is warranted. Consider Skinny Tables and archive policies to keep active datasets performant.

recommendations.title: Org Split Evaluation
recommendations.body: For fundamental scalability challenges: evaluate whether an Org Split (e.g., by business unit or region) is warranted. This is a high-effort initiative, but for mature orgs approaching platform limits, it may be the only long-term solution.

title: Take Along
subtitle: Moderate impact with low effort — include in your next deployment when you're already working in the area.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → API Usage shows callout volume and which Connected Apps generate the most requests.

recommendations.title: Improve Callout Error Handling
recommendations.body: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for external callouts. Use Named Credentials instead of hardcoded endpoints. Log failed callouts to a Custom Object for monitoring and create alerts for sustained error patterns.

recommendations.title: Deactivate Unused Flows & Process Builder
recommendations.body: Identify inactive or redundant Flows and Process Builder automations. Deactivate or delete them to eliminate unnecessary evaluations during DML operations. Each active automation adds overhead to every record save on its trigger object.

title: Evaluate
subtitle: Moderate impact with moderate effort — conduct a detailed evaluation before committing resources.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Login Metrics & Failed Logins reveal authentication issues and potentially unauthorized access attempts.

recommendations.title: Login Failure Root Cause Analysis
recommendations.body: Investigate login failure causes: locked accounts, misconfigured password policies, SSO configuration errors, IP range restrictions. Use Login History and Event Monitoring for root cause analysis. Sustained failed logins can indicate security issues or user experience problems.

recommendations.title: Integration Pattern Review
recommendations.body: For recurring callout errors: review whether the correct integration pattern is in use (Request-Reply vs. Fire-and-Forget vs. Batch). Synchronous callouts are often the root cause of timeout errors. Consider migrating to Platform Events for decoupled processing.

title: Weigh Up
subtitle: Moderate impact with high effort — only pursue with a clear ROI justification and stakeholder alignment.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Concurrent UI Errors indicate frontend performance issues that may point to deeper architectural concerns.

recommendations.title: Lightning Web Components Performance Audit
recommendations.body: Analyze Lightning Web Components for unnecessary re-renders, missing lazy loading, and excessive wire adapters. Use Chrome DevTools Performance tab and the Lightning Inspector browser extension to identify render bottlenecks.

recommendations.title: Aura to LWC Migration
recommendations.body: Identify performance-critical Aura Components and plan migration to Lightning Web Components. LWC delivers significantly better render performance due to its lighter framework overhead and closer-to-native Web Component model.

title: Opportunistic
subtitle: Low impact with low effort — pick up opportunistically when you're already working in the area.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → API Usage Trends show whether API consumption can be optimized.

recommendations.title: Clean Up Unused Custom Fields
recommendations.body: Identify Custom Fields with zero data population using the Field Usage report or third-party metadata tools. Delete or archive unused fields to reduce object complexity, improve developer experience, and speed up describe calls.

recommendations.title: Optimize Debug Log Levels
recommendations.body: Ensure no Debug Logs with Fine or Finest log levels are active in production. Elevated log levels can cause up to 10% CPU overhead per transaction and contribute to Apex CPU Time limit exceptions under load.

title: Defer
subtitle: Low impact with moderate effort — add to the backlog but do not actively prioritize.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center shows no urgent signals for these items — they stem from best-practice reviews and long-term governance goals.

recommendations.title: Increase Apex Test Coverage
recommendations.body: Identify Apex classes with less than 75% code coverage and create meaningful unit tests. Focus on testing critical business logic and edge cases, not just raising the coverage percentage. Use Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest() to properly isolate Governor Limit consumption.

recommendations.title: Migrate from Profiles to Permission Sets
recommendations.body: Migrate security assignments from Profiles to Permission Sets and Permission Set Groups. This reduces maintenance overhead, improves governance, and aligns with Salesforce's long-term direction of deprecating Profile-based permissions.

title: Skip
subtitle: Low impact with high effort — a deliberate decision not to pursue these measures. Document the rationale.
scale_center_hint: These recommendations appear in best-practice guides but deliver insufficient ROI in the context of your specific org's performance profile.

recommendations.title: Complete Apex Layer Rewrite
recommendations.body: A full rewrite of all Apex classes to a new framework (e.g., Enterprise Design Patterns from scratch) sounds appealing but is almost never economically justified in production orgs with mature, tested codebases. Prefer incremental refactoring of hotspots instead.

recommendations.title: Custom UI Replacing Standard Features
recommendations.body: Building custom Lightning Web Components to replace standard features (List Views, Related Lists, Reports) creates significant ongoing maintenance burden. The effort to keep parity with Salesforce's evolving standard features almost always exceeds the benefit.
